devanagari: अथाष्टादशोऽध्यायः । श्रीशुक उवाच । पृश्निस्तु पत्नी सवितुः सावित्रीं व्याहृतिं त्रयीम् । अग्निहोत्रं पशुं सोमं चातुर्मास्यं महामखान् ॥ १...
english_devnagari: athāṣṭādaśo'dhyāyaḥśrīśuka uvācapṛśnistu patnī savituḥ sāvitrīṃ vyāhṛtiṃ trayīmagnihotraṃ paśuṃ somaṃ cāturmāsyaṃ mahāmakhān
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Pṛśni, the wife of Savitṛ (the fifth son of Aditi) gave birth to three daughters, namely, Sāvitrī[1], Vyāhṛti[2] and Trayī[3] and (nine sons, namely,) Agnihotra[4], Paśu[5], Soma[6], Cāturmāsya[7] and the five great sacrifices[8].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The deity presiding over the holy Gāyatrī Mantra,

Footnote [2]:
The deity presiding over the three mystical syllables, viz., Bhūḥ, Bhuvaḥ and Svar usually repeated after OM. These arc prefixed to the Gāyatrī Mantra. They stand for the earth, the intermediate region and the heaven.

Footnote [3]:
The deity presiding over the three Vedas, viz., Ṛgveda, Yajurveda and Sāmaveda.

Footnote [4]:
The deity presiding over the acts of offering oblations into the sacred fire.

Footnote [5]:
The presiding deity of animal sacrifices.

Footnote [6]:
The deity presiding over the Soma Sacrifice.

Footnote [7]:
The presiding deity over the three Cāturmāsya sacrifices performed at the beginning of the three main seasons, viz., the summer, the rainy season and the winter.

Footnote [8]:
The names of the five great sacrifices (Mahāmakhaṇḍa) as follows:
(1) Brahmayajña: Propitiation of the ancient sages. It consists of teaching and chanting the Vedas.
(2) Pitṛyajña: Grateful acknowledgement of our debt to our departed forefathers. This includes tarpaṇa or pouring water as oblations in their memory and for their gratification.
(3) Devayajña: Propitiation of gods through homa (pouring oblations in the sacred fire uttering their names).
(4) Bhūtayajña: Offering a portion of our food to all sub-human beings in acknowledgement of our debt to them.
(5) Nṛyajña: Worship of our fellow beings. It consists of offering food and shelter to an atithi (A traveller staying for only one day).
These five daily sacrifices are binding on every householder (Vide Manu 3-70-71).

devanagari: सिद्धिर्भगस्य भार्याङ्ग महिमानं विभुं प्रभुम् । आशिषं च वरारोहां कन्यां प्रासूत सुव्रताम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: siddhirbhagasya bhāryāṅga mahimānaṃ vibhuṃ prabhumāśiṣaṃ ca varārohāṃ kanyāṃ prāsūta suvratām
translation: Oh dear Parīkṣit! Siddhi, the wife of Bhaga (the sixth son of Aditi), brought forth three sons, namely, Mahiman, Vibhu and Prabhu, and a beautiful pious daughter called Āśis.

devanagari: धातुः कुहूः सिनीवाली राका चानुमतिस्तथा । सायं दर्शमथ प्रातः पूर्णमासमनुक्रमात् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhātuḥ kuhūḥ sinīvālī rākā cānumatistathāsāyaṃ darśamatha prātaḥ pūrṇamāsamanukramāt
translation: Out of the four wives of Dhātṛ (the seventh son of Aditi) Kuhū[9] bore Sāyam[10]; Sinīvālī[11], Darśa[12]; Rākā[13], Prātar[14]; Anumati[15], Pūrṇamāsa[16] respectively.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
The deity presiding over the last night of a dark fortnight.

Footnote [10]:
The presiding deity over the evening.

Footnote [11]:
The deity presiding over the 14th night of a dark fortnight.

Footnote [12]:
The presiding deity over the last day of a dark fortnight.

Footnote [13]:
The presiding deity of the last night of the bright fortnight.

Footnote [14]:
The presiding deity of the morning.

Footnote [15]:
The deity presiding over the 14th night pf a bright fortnight.

Footnote [16]:
The deity presiding over the last day of a bright fortnight.

devanagari: अग्नीन्पुरीष्यानाधत्त क्रियायां समनन्तरः । चर्षणी वरुणस्यासीद्यस्यां जातो भृगुः पुनः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: agnīnpurīṣyānādhatta kriyāyāṃ samanantaraḥcarṣaṇī varuṇasyāsīdyasyāṃ jāto bhṛguḥ punaḥ
translation: Samanantara or Vidhātṛ (the eighth son of Aditi) begot on Kriyā the Fire-gods called Purīṣya[17]. Carṣaṇī was the spouse of Varuṇa (the ninth son of Aditi. He is the deity presiding over water). To her was reborn Bhṛgu (the son of Brahmā).
purport:
Footnote [17]:
The five deities presiding over the sacred fires known as Purīṣya.

devanagari: वाल्मीकिश्च महायोगी वल्मीकादभवत्किल । अगस्त्यश्च वसिष्ठश्च मित्रावरुणयोरृषी ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: vālmīkiśca mahāyogī valmīkādabhavatkilaagastyaśca vasiṣṭhaśca mitrāvaruṇayorṛṣī
translation: Vālmīki, another son of Varuṇa, was a great Yogī. They say that he was born from an ant-hill. The sages Agastya and Vasiṣṭha were born of Mitra and Varuṇa who, in a jar, deposited their semen that was discharged at the sight of and in the vicinity of the celestial damsel Urvaśī. Mitra (the 10th son of Aditi) begot through Revatī three sons, namely, Utsarga, Ariṣṭa, and Pippala.

devanagari: रेतः सिषिचतुः कुम्भे उर्वश्याः सन्निधौ द्रुतम् । रेवत्यां मित्र उत्सर्गमरिष्टं पिप्पलं व्यधात् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: retaḥ siṣicatuḥ kumbhe urvaśyāḥ sannidhau drutamrevatyāṃ mitra utsargamariṣṭaṃ pippalaṃ vyadhāt
translation: Vālmīki, another son of Varuṇa, was a great Yogī. They say that he was born from an ant-hill. The sages Agastya and Vasiṣṭha were born of Mitra and Varuṇa who, in a jar, deposited their semen that was discharged at the sight of and in the vicinity of the celestial damsel Urvaśī. Mitra (the 10th son of Aditi) begot through Revatī three sons, namely, Utsarga, Ariṣṭa, and Pippala.

devanagari: पौलोम्यामिन्द्र आधत्त त्रीन्पुत्रानिति नः श्रुतम् । जयन्तमृषभं तात तृतीयं मीढुषं प्रभुः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: paulomyāmindra ādhatta trīnputrāniti naḥ śrutamjayantamṛṣabhaṃ tāta tṛtīyaṃ mīḍhuṣaṃ prabhuḥ
translation: We are told that the king of gods, Indra, (the 11th son of Aditi) procreated through Paulomī or Śacī three sons, namely, Jayanta, Ṛṣabha and the third Mīḍhusa, Oh child!

devanagari: उरुक्रमस्य देवस्य मायावामनरूपिणः । कीर्तौ पत्न्यां बृहच्छ्लोकस्तस्यासन्सौभगादयः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: urukramasya devasya māyāvāmanarūpiṇaḥkīrtau patnyāṃ bṛhacchlokastasyāsansaubhagādayaḥ
translation: Urukrama who was god Viṣṇu who appeared as Vāmana through his own divine Māyā (and who was the 12th son of Aditi) begot on his wife Kīrti, a son called Bṛhacchloka who was the father of Saubhaga and other sons.

devanagari: तत्कर्मगुणवीर्याणि काश्यपस्य महात्मनः । पश्चाद्वक्ष्यामहेऽदित्यां यथैवावततार ह ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatkarmaguṇavīryāṇi kāśyapasya mahātmanaḥpaścādvakṣyāmahe'dityāṃ yathaivāvatatāra ha
translation: We shall narrate in detail later (in Skandha VIII) the feats, excellences and the deeds of the high-souled son of Kaśyapa, namely, Vāmana, and also how he incarnated as the son of Aditi.

devanagari: अथ कश्यपदायादान्दैतेयान्कीर्तयामि ते । यत्र भागवतः श्रीमान्प्रह्रादो बलिरेव च ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: atha kaśyapadāyādāndaiteyānkīrtayāmi teyatra bhāgavataḥ śrīmānprahrādo balireva ca
translation: Now, I shall describe to you the sons of the sage Kaśyapa from his spouse Diti. Among them were born the great devotees of Viṣṇu, namely, the glorious Prahlāda and Bali.

devanagari: दितेर्द्वावेव दायादौ दैत्यदानववन्दितौ । हिरण्यकशिपुर्नाम हिरण्याक्षश्च कीर्तितौ ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: diterdvāveva dāyādau daityadānavavanditauhiraṇyakaśipurnāma hiraṇyākṣaśca kīrtitau
translation: Diti had two sons, namely, Hiraṇyakaśipu and Hiraṇyākṣa who were respected by Daityas and Dānavas. They were described (formerly in Skandha III).

devanagari: हिरण्यकशिपोर्भार्या कयाधुर्नाम दानवी । जम्भस्य तनया सा तु सुषुवे चतुरः सुतान् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: hiraṇyakaśiporbhāryā kayādhurnāma dānavījambhasya tanayā sā tu suṣuve caturaḥ sutān
translation: The wife of Hiraṇyakaśipu was a Dānava lady called Kayādhu. She was the daughter of Jambha and was given in marriage to Hiraṇyakaśipu. She gave birth to hour sons, namely, Saṃhrāda the elder-most, and Anuhrāda, Hrāda and Prahlāda. Their sister was Siṃhikā by name. She got from Vipracittia son called Rāhu whose head was lopped off by Hari with his disc (Sudarśana) while he (Rāhu) was drinking ambrosia. Kṛti the wife of Saṃhrāda gave birth to Pañcajana. Dhamanī the wife of Hrāda bore Vātāpi and Ilvala. It was Ilvala who cooked Vātāpi for the celebrated guest Agastya.

devanagari: संह्रादं प्रागनुह्रादं ह्रादं प्रह्रादमेव च । तत्स्वसा सिंहिका नाम राहुं विप्रचितोऽग्रहीत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃhrādaṃ prāganuhrādaṃ hrādaṃ prahrādameva catatsvasā siṃhikā nāma rāhuṃ vipracito'grahīt

devanagari: शिरोऽहरद्यस्य हरिश्चक्रेण पिबतोऽमृतम् । संह्रादस्य कृतिर्भार्या सूत पञ्चजनं ततः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śiro'haradyasya hariścakreṇa pibato'mṛtamsaṃhrādasya kṛtirbhāryā sūta pañcajanaṃ tataḥ

devanagari: ह्रादस्य धमनिर्भार्या सूत वातापिमिल्वलम् । योऽगस्त्याय त्वतिथये पेचे वातापिमिल्वलः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: hrādasya dhamanirbhāryā sūta vātāpimilvalamyo'gastyāya tvatithaye pece vātāpimilvalaḥ
translation: The wife of Hiraṇyakaśipu was a Dānava lady called Kayādhu. She was the daughter of Jambha and was given in marriage to Hiraṇyakaśipu. She gave birth to hour sons, namely, Saṃhrāda the elder-most, and Anuhrāda, Hrāda and Prahlāda. Their sister was Siṃhikā by name. She got from Vipracittia son called Rāhu whose head was lopped off by Hari with his disc (Sudarśana) while he (Rāhu) was drinking ambrosia. Kṛti the wife of Saṃhrāda gave birth to Pañcajana. Dhamanī the wife of Hrāda bore Vātāpi and Ilvala. It was Ilvala who cooked Vātāpi for the celebrated guest Agastya.

devanagari: अनुह्रादस्य सूर्यायां१ बाष्कलो महिषस्तथा । विरोचनस्तु प्राह्रादिर्देव्यां तस्याभवद्बलिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: anuhrādasya sūryāyāṃ1 bāṣkalo mahiṣastathāvirocanastu prāhrādirdevyāṃ tasyābhavadbaliḥ
translation: Anuhrāda brought forth through his wife Sūrmyā two sons Bāṣkala and Mahiṣa. The son of Prahlāda was Virocana who had from his spouse Devī, a son called Bali.

devanagari: बाणज्येष्ठं पुत्रशतमशनायां ततोऽभवत् । तस्यानुभावं सुश्लोक्यं पश्चादेवाभिधास्यते ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: bāṇajyeṣṭhaṃ putraśatamaśanāyāṃ tato'bhavattasyānubhāvaṃ suślokyaṃ paścādevābhidhāsyate
translation: From Bali, a hundred sons out of whom Bāṇa was the eldest, were born to (Bali’s queen) Aśanā. We shall recount later on (in Skandha VIII) the glory of Bali which deserves celebration.

devanagari: बाण आराध्य गिरिशं लेभे तद्गणमुख्यताम् । यत्पार्श्वे भगवानास्ते ह्यद्यापि पुरपालकः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bāṇa ārādhya giriśaṃ lebhe tadgaṇamukhyatāmyatpārśve bhagavānāste hyadyāpi purapālakaḥ
translation: Having propitiated god Śiva, Bāṇa attained the first place among his attendants. The glorious god Śiva is by his side and He is still guarding his capital city.

devanagari: मरुतश्च दितेः पुत्राश्चत्वारिंशन्नवाधिकाः । त आसन्नप्रजाः सर्वे नीता इन्द्रे ण सात्मताम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: marutaśca diteḥ putrāścatvāriṃśannavādhikāḥta āsannaprajāḥ sarve nītā indre ṇa sātmatām
translation: Diti had sons called Maruts. They were forty-nine in number. All of them were childless and they were brought over by Indra and were converted to his own class of gods.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । कथं त आसुरं भावमपोह्यौत्पत्तिकं गुरो । इन्द्रे ण प्रापिताः सात्म्यं किं तत्साधु कृतं हि तैः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācakathaṃ ta āsuraṃ bhāvamapohyautpattikaṃ guroindre ṇa prāpitāḥ sātmyaṃ kiṃ tatsādhu kṛtaṃ hi taiḥ
translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) said:
Oh Preceptor, how did Indra convert their inborn demonic nature to godhood? What obligations did they render to Indra which made him convert them into gods.

devanagari: इमे श्रद्दधते ब्रह्मन्नृषयो हि मया सह । परिज्ञानाय भगवंस्तन्नो व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ime śraddadhate brahmannṛṣayo hi mayā sahaparijñānāya bhagavaṃstanno vyākhyātumarhasi
translation: Oh worshipful sage, these other sages along with me, earnestly desire to know the details. You should therefore expound that to us.

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । तद्विष्णुरातस्य स बादरायणिर्वचो निशम्यादृतमल्पमर्थवत् । सभाजयन्सन्निभृतेन चेतसा जगाद सत्रायण सर्वदर्शनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīsūta uvācatadviṣṇurātasya sa bādarāyaṇirvaco niśamyādṛtamalpamarthavatsabhājayansannibhṛtena cetasā jagāda satrāyaṇa sarvadarśanaḥ
translation:
Sūta said:
Oh Śaunaka, having heard that speech of Viṣṇurāta (king Parīkṣit), Śuka, the son of sage Bādarāyaṇa (Vyāsa), greeted it with heart full of joy, as it was full of earnestness and was brief yet pregnant with significance. The Omniscient Śuka spoke as follows:

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । हतपुत्रा दितिः शक्र पार्ष्णिग्राहेण विष्णुना । मन्युना शोकदीप्तेन ज्वलन्ती पर्यचिन्तयत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācahataputrā ditiḥ śakra pārṣṇigrāheṇa viṣṇunāmanyunā śokadīptena jvalantī paryacintayat
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Diti whose sons were slain by Viṣṇu as the ally of Indra, in the background, was burning with wrath enkindled by grief and she began to ponder.

devanagari: कदा नु भ्रातृहन्तारमिन्द्रि याराममुल्बणम् । अक्लिन्नहृदयं पापं घातयित्वा शये सुखम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: kadā nu bhrātṛhantāramindri yārāmamulbaṇamaklinnahṛdayaṃ pāpaṃ ghātayitvā śaye sukham
translation: “When can I get happy sleep after bringing about the death of the hard-hearted wicked sensual Indra, the cruel murderer of his brothers.

devanagari: कृमिविड्भस्मसंज्ञासीद्यस्येशाभिहितस्य च । भूतध्रुक्तत्कृते स्वार्थं किं वेद निरयो यतः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛmiviḍbhasmasaṃjñāsīdyasyeśābhihitasya cabhūtadhruktatkṛte svārthaṃ kiṃ veda nirayo yataḥ
translation: The body which is addressed as “A Lord or a Ruler”, goes also under the name of worms (becomes full of worms after burial), excrement (if the body is eaten up by carnivorous animals or birds of prey), and ashes (if it is cremated). He (Indra) who seeks to injure other beings for such a body, does not know self-interest, for it leads to hell.

devanagari: आशासानस्य तस्येदं ध्रुवमुन्नद्धचेतसः । मदशोषक इन्द्र स्य भूयाद्येन सुतो हि मे ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: āśāsānasya tasyedaṃ dhruvamunnaddhacetasaḥmadaśoṣaka indra sya bhūyādyena suto hi me
translation: By what means can I get a son who will quell the pride of Indra who wrongly presumes that this body is eternal and who is very arrogant (of unrestrained heart)?”

devanagari: इति भावेन सा भर्तुराचचारासकृत्प्रियम् । शुश्रूषयानुरागेण प्रश्रयेण दमेन च ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: iti bhāvena sā bharturācacārāsakṛtpriyamśuśrūṣayānurāgeṇa praśrayeṇa damena ca
translation: With this object in view, she many times served what was wished by her husband. By her service, affection, modesty, self-control, intense devotion, by her charming sweet words as well as by her winning smiles and side glances, she who was intimate with the knowledge of human nature, captivated his heart.

devanagari: भक्त्या परमया राजन्मनोज्ञैर्वल्गुभाषितैः । मनो जग्राह भावज्ञा सस्मितापाङ्गवीक्षणैः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktyā paramayā rājanmanojñairvalgubhāṣitaiḥmano jagrāha bhāvajñā sasmitāpāṅgavīkṣaṇaiḥ
translation: With this object in view, she many times served what was wished by her husband. By her service, affection, modesty, self-control, intense devotion, by her charming sweet words as well as by her winning smiles and side glances, she who was intimate with the knowledge of human nature, captivated his heart.

devanagari: एवं स्त्रिया जडीभूतो विद्वानपि मनोज्ञया । बाढमित्याह विवशो न तच्चित्रं हि योषिति ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ striyā jaḍībhūto vidvānapi manojñayābāḍhamityāha vivaśo na taccitraṃ hi yoṣiti
translation: Thus enchanted by the sly, clever woman, Kaśyapa, though learned, became powerless (against her charms). He succumbed to her and acquiesced in her request. Such stupefaction by women is not to be wondered at.

devanagari: विलोक्यैकान्तभूतानि भूतान्यादौ प्रजापतिः । स्त्रियं चक्रे स्वदेहार्धं यया पुंसां मतिर्हृता ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: vilokyaikāntabhūtāni bhūtānyādau prajāpatiḥstriyaṃ cakre svadehārdhaṃ yayā puṃsāṃ matirhṛtā
translation: At the beginning of creation, Prajāpati found that beings entertained no attachment (and the work of creation was not possible), god Brahmā (then) converted half of his body into a woman who leads astray the judgments of men.

devanagari: एवं शुश्रूषितस्तात भगवान्कश्यपः स्त्रिया । प्रहस्य परमप्रीतो दितिमाहाभिनन्द्य च ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ śuśrūṣitastāta bhagavānkaśyapaḥ striyāprahasya paramaprīto ditimāhābhinandya ca
translation: The worshipful Kaśyapa who was thus waited upon by his wife, was highly pleased with Diti. With a hearty laugh, he greeted Diti with delight (and said).

devanagari: श्रीकश्यप उवाच । वरं वरय वामोरु प्रीतस्तेऽहमनिन्दिते । स्त्रिया भर्तरि सुप्रीते कः काम इह चागमः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīkaśyapa uvācavaraṃ varaya vāmoru prītaste'hamaninditestriyā bhartari suprīte kaḥ kāma iha cāgamaḥ
translation:
Kaśyapa said:
Ask for a boon Oh beautiful lady with shapely thighs. Oh faultless one, I am pleased with you. When husband is pleased with his wife, what object in this or in the next world, is unattainable?

devanagari: पतिरेव हि नारीणां दैवतं परमं स्मृतम् । मानसः सर्वभूतानां वासुदेवः श्रियः पतिः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: patireva hi nārīṇāṃ daivataṃ paramaṃ smṛtammānasaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ vāsudevaḥ śriyaḥ patiḥ
translation: It has been declared that the husband alone is the supreme deity in the case of women.

devanagari: स एव देवतालिङ्गैर्नामरूपविकल्पितैः । इज्यते भगवान्पुम्भिः स्त्रीभिश्च पतिरूपधृक् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva devatāliṅgairnāmarūpavikalpitaiḥijyate bhagavānpumbhiḥ strībhiśca patirūpadhṛk
translation:
It has been declared that the husband alone is the supreme deity in the case of women.
For the glorious Lord Vāsudeva, the husband of the goddess Śrī, who is in the heart of all beings, is worshipped in the guise of different deities by men who ascribe to him different names (e.g. Indra, Varuṇa) and forms (wielder of the Vajra, or of a noose). But he is worshipped by women in the form of their husbands.

devanagari: तस्मात्पतिव्रता नार्यः श्रेयस्कामाः सुमध्यमे । यजन्तेऽनन्यभावेन पतिमात्मानमीश्वरम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātpativratā nāryaḥ śreyaskāmāḥ sumadhyameyajante'nanyabhāvena patimātmānamīśvaram
translation: Therefore, Oh lady of beautiful waist, chaste women who are desirous of their welfare, worship with unswerving devotion their husband as the image of god and the Soul of the world.

devanagari: सोऽहं त्वयार्चितो भद्रे ईदृग्भावेन भक्तितः । तं ते सम्पादये काममसतीनां सुदुर्लभम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: so'haṃ tvayārcito bhadre īdṛgbhāvena bhaktitaḥtaṃ te sampādaye kāmamasatīnāṃ sudurlabham
translation: I who am worshipped by you with so much devotion and with such superb faith, shall accomplish for you your desired object, Oh blessed lady—an object which is very difficult to be attained by unchaste women.

devanagari: दितिरुवाच । वरदो यदि मे ब्रह्मन्पुत्रमिन्द्र हणं वृणे । अमृत्युं मृतपुत्राहं येन मे घातितौ सुतौ ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: ditiruvācavarado yadi me brahmanputramindra haṇaṃ vṛṇeamṛtyuṃ mṛtaputrāhaṃ yena me ghātitau sutau
translation:
Diti said:
“Oh Brāhmaṇa sage, if you are conferring a boon on me, I pray for an immortal son who will slay Indra, for he (Indra) caused both of my sons killed and I have become a lady whose sons are dead.”

devanagari: निशम्य तद्वचो विप्रो विमनाः पर्यतप्यत । अहो अधर्मः सुमहानद्य मे समुपस्थितः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: niśamya tadvaco vipro vimanāḥ paryatapyataaho adharmaḥ sumahānadya me samupasthitaḥ
translation: Hearing her speech, the Brāhmaṇa became dispirited and repented (as follows): “Alas! What a great unrighteousness and sin have overtaken me today!

devanagari: अहो अर्थेन्द्रि यारामो योषिन्मय्येह मायया । गृहीतचेताः कृपणः पतिष्ये नरके ध्रुवम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: aho arthendri yārāmo yoṣinmayyeha māyayāgṛhītacetāḥ kṛpaṇaḥ patiṣye narake dhruvam
translation: Alas! Today I have fallen a victim to sense gratification. I allowed my heart to be captivated by Māyā (deluding power) in the form of a woman. Vile as I am, I shall certainly fall in the abysmal hell.

devanagari: कोऽतिक्रमोऽनुवर्तन्त्याः स्वभावमिह योषितः । धिङ्मां बताबुधं स्वार्थे यदहं त्वजितेन्द्रि यः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ko'tikramo'nuvartantyāḥ svabhāvamiha yoṣitaḥdhiṅmāṃ batābudhaṃ svārthe yadahaṃ tvajitendri yaḥ
translation: What fault is there in the case of the woman who followed the natural tendencies of women? Alas! Fie upon me who am ignorant about my self-interest and lost the control of my senses.

devanagari: शरत्पद्मोत्सवं वक्त्रं वचश्च श्रवणामृतम् । हृदयं क्षुरधाराभं स्त्रीणां को वेद चेष्टितम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: śaratpadmotsavaṃ vaktraṃ vacaśca śravaṇāmṛtamhṛdayaṃ kṣuradhārābhaṃ strīṇāṃ ko veda ceṣṭitam
translation: Who can fathom the motives of the actions of women whose countenance is beautiful like the fully bloomed autumnal lotus, whose speech is like ambrosia to the ear but whose heart is sharp like a razor-blade.

devanagari: न हि कश्चित्प्रियः स्त्रीणामञ्जसा स्वाशिषात्मनाम् । पतिं पुत्रं भ्रातरं वा घ्नन्त्यर्थे घातयन्ति च ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: na hi kaścitpriyaḥ strīṇāmañjasā svāśiṣātmanāmpatiṃ putraṃ bhrātaraṃ vā ghnantyarthe ghātayanti ca
translation: Women whose minds are determined to achieve their own objective have no one really dear. For serving their own purpose, they will kill or cause to be killed their husband, son or brother.

devanagari: प्रतिश्रुतं ददामीति वचस्तन्न मृषा भवेत् । वधं नार्हति चेन्द्रो ऽपि तत्रेदमुपकल्पते ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: pratiśrutaṃ dadāmīti vacastanna mṛṣā bhavetvadhaṃ nārhati cendro 'pi tatredamupakalpate
translation: I have promised that I shall give her a son. That promise should not prove untrue. But Indra does not deserve death. Under these circumstances the following expedient appears proper.

devanagari: इति सञ्चिन्त्य भगवान्मारीचः कुरुनन्दन । उवाच किञ्चित्कुपित आत्मानं च विगर्हयन् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sañcintya bhagavānmārīcaḥ kurunandanauvāca kiñcitkupita ātmānaṃ ca vigarhayan
translation: Oh descend, nt of the Kurus! Considering thus, the worshipful son of Marīci (Kaśyapa) showed some indignation and condemning himself said.

devanagari: श्रीकश्यप उवाच । पुत्रस्ते भविता भद्रे इन्द्र हादेवबान्धवः । संवत्सरं व्रतमिदं यद्यञ्जो धारयिष्यसि ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīkaśyapa uvācaputraste bhavitā bhadre indra hādevabāndhavaḥsaṃvatsaraṃ vratamidaṃ yadyañjo dhārayiṣyasi
translation:
Kaśyapa said:
Oh blessed lady, if you carefully observe this vow for one complete year, a son will be born to you who will be the slayer of Indra, or a friend of gods (if this vow is not properly observed).

devanagari: दितिरुवाच । धारयिष्ये व्रतं ब्रह्मन्ब्रूहि कार्याणि यानि मे । यानि चेह निषिद्धानि न व्रतं घ्नन्ति यान्युत ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ditiruvācadhārayiṣye vrataṃ brahmanbrūhi kāryāṇi yāni meyāni ceha niṣiddhāni na vrataṃ ghnanti yānyuta
translation:
Diti said:
Oh Brāhmaṇa sage! I hereby undertake to observe the vow. Explain to me what acts are obligatory and what are prohibited for me, and also the acts which (are neither obligatory nor prohibitive but which) do not violate the vow.

devanagari: श्रीकश्यप उवाच । न हिंस्याद्भूतजातानि न शपेन्नानृतं वदेत् । न छिन्द्यान्नखरोमाणि न स्पृशेद्यदमङ्गलम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīkaśyapa uvācana hiṃsyādbhūtajātāni na śapennānṛtaṃ vadetna chindyānnakharomāṇi na spṛśedyadamaṅgalam
translation:
Kaśyapa said:
You should not injure any of the living beings. You should not curse or tell a lie. You should not pare your nails or cut your hair. You should not touch an impure and inauspicious thing.

devanagari: नाप्सु स्नायान्न कुप्येत न सम्भाषेत दुर्जनैः । न वसीताधौतवासः स्रजं च विधृतां क्वचित् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: nāpsu snāyānna kupyeta na sambhāṣeta durjanaiḥna vasītādhautavāsaḥ srajaṃ ca vidhṛtāṃ kvacit
translation: You should not take bath into water by plunging in it. You should not get angry or speak with wicked men. You should not put on unwashed garments nor should wear a garland worn by others.

devanagari: नोच्छिष्टं चण्डिकान्नं च सामिषं वृषलाहृतम् । भुञ्जीतोदक्यया दृष्टं पिबेन्नाञ्जलिना त्वपः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: nocchiṣṭaṃ caṇḍikānnaṃ ca sāmiṣaṃ vṛṣalāhṛtambhuñjītodakyayā dṛṣṭaṃ pibennāñjalinā tvapaḥ
translation: You should not eat food which has remained after being eaten by others; nor food offered to the goddess Bhad- rakālī or to seven Mātṛkās or eaten by ants. You should not eat nonvegetarian food or food brought by a Śūdra. You should not take food which is seen by a woman in monthly course. You should not drink water with your folded palms.

devanagari: नोच्छिष्टास्पृष्टसलिला सन्ध्यायां मुक्तमूर्धजा । अनर्चितासंयतवाक्नासंवीता बहिश्चरेत् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: nocchiṣṭāspṛṣṭasalilā sandhyāyāṃ muktamūrdhajāanarcitāsaṃyatavāknāsaṃvītā bahiścaret
translation: You must not go out with remnants of food sticking in the mouth or without rinsing the mouth with water. You should not stir out in the evening with dishevelled hair, unadorned or without the upper garment. You should not be unrestrained in speech.

devanagari: नाधौतपादाप्रयता नार्द्र पादा उदक्शिराः । शयीत नापराङ्नान्यैर्न नग्ना न च सन्ध्ययोः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: nādhautapādāprayatā nārdra pādā udakśirāḥśayīta nāparāṅnānyairna nagnā na ca sandhyayoḥ
translation: You should not sleep without washing your feet or in an impure condition or with wet feet. (You should not go to bed) with your head to the North or to the West. You should not sleep with others or without any clothes. You should not remain asleep at dawn and dusk.

devanagari: धौतवासा शुचिर्नित्यं सर्वमङ्गलसंयुता । पूजयेत्प्रातराशात्प्राग्गोविप्राञ्श्रियमच्युतम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: dhautavāsā śucirnityaṃ sarvamaṅgalasaṃyutāpūjayetprātarāśātprāggoviprāñśriyamacyutam
translation: Putting on washed garments, always pure and furnished with all auspicious things, you should worship god Viṣṇu, goddess Śrī, cows and Brāhmaṇas before you take breakfast in the morning.

devanagari: स्त्रियो वीरवतीश्चार्चेत्स्रग्गन्धबलिमण्डनैः । पतिं चार्च्योपतिष्ठेत ध्यायेत्कोष्ठगतं च तम् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: striyo vīravatīścārcetsraggandhabalimaṇḍanaiḥpatiṃ cārcyopatiṣṭheta dhyāyetkoṣṭhagataṃ ca tam
translation: You should worship women whose husbands are alive, by offering them garlands, sandal-paste, food and ornaments. Having worshipped your husband, you should wait upon him and contemplate that your husband’s energy is present in your womb.

devanagari: सांवत्सरं पुंसवनं व्रतमेतदविप्लुतम् । धारयिष्यसि चेत्तुभ्यं शक्रहा भविता सुतः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: sāṃvatsaraṃ puṃsavanaṃ vratametadaviplutamdhārayiṣyasi cettubhyaṃ śakrahā bhavitā sutaḥ
translation: If you will observe this vow called Puṃsavana (yielder of a son) for the period of one year without any flaw, you will beget a son who will be the slayer of Indra.

devanagari: बाढमित्यभ्युपेत्याथ दिती राजन्महामनाः । कश्यपाद्गर्भमाधत्त व्रतं चाञ्जो दधार सा ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: bāḍhamityabhyupetyātha ditī rājanmahāmanāḥkaśyapādgarbhamādhatta vrataṃ cāñjo dadhāra sā
translation: Diti agreed to that. She conceived from Kaśyapa and strictly (began to) observe the vow, Oh king.

devanagari: मातृष्वसुरभिप्रायमिन्द्र आज्ञाय मानद । शुश्रूषणेनाश्रमस्थां दितिं पर्यचरत्कविः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: mātṛṣvasurabhiprāyamindra ājñāya mānadaśuśrūṣaṇenāśramasthāṃ ditiṃ paryacaratkaviḥ
translation: Having come to know the intention of his maternal aunt the sly foresighted Indra waited upon Diti who was staying in a hermitage, (under the pretext of) rendering service to her.

devanagari: नित्यं वनात्सुमनसः फलमूलसमित्कुशान् । पत्राङ्कुरमृदोऽपश्च काले काल उपाहरत् ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: nityaṃ vanātsumanasaḥ phalamūlasamitkuśānpatrāṅkuramṛdo'paśca kāle kāla upāharat
translation: At the appointed time he always brought flowers, fruits, sacred sticks, kuśagrass, leaves, shoots, earth and water from the forest.

devanagari: एवं तस्या व्रतस्थाया व्रतच्छिद्रं हरिर्नृप । प्रेप्सुः पर्यचरज्जिह्मो मृगहेव मृगाकृतिः ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ tasyā vratasthāyā vratacchidraṃ harirnṛpaprepsuḥ paryacarajjihmo mṛgaheva mṛgākṛtiḥ
translation: Oh King, in this way the crooked Indra, desiring to find out some flaw in the course of the observance of vows of the lady who had undertaken them, served her like a hunter who puts on the guise of a deer.

devanagari: नाध्यगच्छद्व्रतच्छिद्रं तत्परोऽथ महीपते । चिन्तां तीव्रां गतः शक्रः केन मे स्याच्छिवं त्विह ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: nādhyagacchadvratacchidraṃ tatparo'tha mahīpatecintāṃ tīvrāṃ gataḥ śakraḥ kena me syācchivaṃ tviha
translation: Indra who was intent on detecting a weak point in her observance of vows, could not find it. He became intensely anxious as to how he could succeed (in securing his good).

devanagari: एकदा सा तु सन्ध्यायामुच्छिष्टा व्रतकर्शिता । अस्पृष्टवार्यधौताङ्घ्रिः सुष्वाप विधिमोहिता ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā sā tu sandhyāyāmucchiṣṭā vratakarśitāaspṛṣṭavāryadhautāṅghriḥ suṣvāpa vidhimohitā
translation: Once in the evening, Diti who was fatigued with the observance of that austere vow and deluded by fate, slept with the remnants of food in the mouth (without rinsing her mouth with water), and with unwashed feet.

devanagari: लब्ध्वा तदन्तरं शक्रो निद्रा पहृतचेतसः । दितेः प्रविष्ट उदरं योगेशो योगमायया ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhvā tadantaraṃ śakro nidrā pahṛtacetasaḥditeḥ praviṣṭa udaraṃ yogeśo yogamāyayā
translation: Getting this opportunity, Indra who was expert in Yoga entered the womb of Diti by his deluding power of Yoga, while Diti was unconscious in sleep.

devanagari: चकर्त सप्तधा गर्भं वज्रेण कनकप्रभम् । रुदन्तं सप्तधैकैकं मा रोदीरिति तान्पुनः ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: cakarta saptadhā garbhaṃ vajreṇa kanakaprabhamrudantaṃ saptadhaikaikaṃ mā rodīriti tānpunaḥ
translation: With his Vajra he cut in seven parts the foetus which was brilliant like gold. While it was crying he said ‘Don’t cry,” and cut each of that piece into seven again.

devanagari: तमूचुः पाट्यमानास्ते सर्वे प्राञ्जलयो नृप । किं न इन्द्र जिघांससि भ्रातरो मरुतस्तव ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: tamūcuḥ pāṭyamānāste sarve prāñjalayo nṛpakiṃ na indra jighāṃsasi bhrātaro marutastava
translation: While they were being rent, all of them folded their palms and appealed to him “Oh Indra, we are Maruts, your brothers. Why do you wish to kill us?”

devanagari: मा भैष्ट भ्रातरो मह्यं यूयमित्याह कौशिकः । अनन्यभावान्पार्षदानात्मनो मरुतां गणान् ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: mā bhaiṣṭa bhrātaro mahyaṃ yūyamityāha kauśikaḥananyabhāvānpārṣadānātmano marutāṃ gaṇān
translation: “Don’t be afraid, Brothers, you are for me”, said Indra to the Maruts who as a class had unswerving loyalty to him as his attendants.

devanagari: न ममार दितेर्गर्भः श्रीनिवासानुकम्पया । बहुधा कुलिशक्षुण्णो द्रौ ण्यस्त्रेण यथा भवान् ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: na mamāra ditergarbhaḥ śrīnivāsānukampayābahudhā kuliśakṣuṇṇo drau ṇyastreṇa yathā bhavān
translation: The foetus in the womb of Diti did not die through the grace of Lord Viṣṇu even though it was repeatedly hacked with Vajra, just as you were saved from the missile of Aśvatthāman.

devanagari: सकृदिष्ट्वादिपुरुषं पुरुषो याति साम्यताम् । संवत्सरं किञ्चिदूनं दित्या यद्धरिरर्चितः ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: sakṛdiṣṭvādipuruṣaṃ puruṣo yāti sāmyatāmsaṃvatsaraṃ kiñcidūnaṃ dityā yaddharirarcitaḥ
translation: If a person worships but once the Ancient Person Viṣṇu, he attains to the self-same form as that of Viṣṇu, whereas the great god Hari was worshipped by Diti for a period slightly less than one year.

devanagari: सजूरिन्द्रे ण पञ्चाशद्देवास्ते मरुतोऽभवन् । व्यपोह्य मातृदोषं ते हरिणा सोमपाः कृताः ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: sajūrindre ṇa pañcāśaddevāste maruto'bhavanvyapohya mātṛdoṣaṃ te hariṇā somapāḥ kṛtāḥ
translation: Along with Indra the Maruts became in all fifty gods. Removing the stigma of maternal heredity, they were made participants in Soma-drinking by Indra.

devanagari: दितिरुत्थाय ददृशे कुमाराननलप्रभान् । इन्द्रे ण सहितान्देवी पर्यतुष्यदनिन्दिता ॥ ६८ ॥
english_devnagari: ditirutthāya dadṛśe kumārānanalaprabhānindre ṇa sahitāndevī paryatuṣyadaninditā
translation: After getting up, Dili saw her sons brilliant like fire in the company of Indra. The faultless lady felt highly- gratified.

devanagari: अथेन्द्र माह ताताहमादित्यानां भयावहम् । अपत्यमिच्छन्त्यचरं व्रतमेतत्सुदुष्करम् ॥ ६९ ॥
english_devnagari: athendra māha tātāhamādityānāṃ bhayāvahamapatyamicchantyacaraṃ vratametatsuduṣkaram
translation: She asked Indra, “Child, desiring a son which would strike terror in the sons of Aditi, I observed this vow which was most difficult.

devanagari: एकः सङ्कल्पितः पुत्रः सप्त सप्ताभवन्कथम् । यदि ते विदितं पुत्र सत्यं कथय मा मृषा ॥ ७० ॥
english_devnagari: ekaḥ saṅkalpitaḥ putraḥ sapta saptābhavankathamyadi te viditaṃ putra satyaṃ kathaya mā mṛṣā
translation: I expected to have one son. How is it that I got seven times seven (foṛty-nine)? Oh Son, if the truth is known to you, please tell me, but don’t tell a lie.”

devanagari: इन्द्र उवाच । अम्ब तेऽहं व्यवसितमुपधार्यागतोऽन्तिकम् । लब्धान्तरोऽच्छिदं गर्भमर्थबुद्धिर्न धर्मदृक् ॥ ७१ ॥
english_devnagari: indra uvācaamba te'haṃ vyavasitamupadhāryāgato'ntikamlabdhāntaro'cchidaṃ garbhamarthabuddhirna dharmadṛk
translation:
Indra said:
Oh mother, having learnt about your determination, I approached you. Detecting a flaw in the observance of your vow, I who did not care for righteousness and was bent on achieving my object, slashed the foetus.

devanagari: कृत्तो मे सप्तधा गर्भ आसन्सप्त कुमारकाः । तेऽपि चैकैकशो वृक्णाः सप्तधा नापि मम्रिरे ॥ ७२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtto me saptadhā garbha āsansapta kumārakāḥte'pi caikaikaśo vṛkṇāḥ saptadhā nāpi mamrire
translation: I hacked the foetus in seven ways and there were seven sons. Each of them was cut in seven; but they did not die.

devanagari: ततस्तत्परमाश्चर्यं वीक्ष्य व्यवसितं मया । महापुरुषपूजायाः सिद्धिः काप्यानुषङ्गिणी ॥ ७३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastatparamāścaryaṃ vīkṣya vyavasitaṃ mayāmahāpuruṣapūjāyāḥ siddhiḥ kāpyānuṣaṅgiṇī
translation: Having seen that greatest miracle, I came to the conclusion that this must be some indescribable incidental Siddhi achieved through the worship of Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: आराधनं भगवत ईहमाना निराशिषः । ये तु नेच्छन्त्यपि परं ते स्वार्थकुशलाः स्मृताः ॥ ७४ ॥
english_devnagari: ārādhanaṃ bhagavata īhamānā nirāśiṣaḥye tu necchantyapi paraṃ te svārthakuśalāḥ smṛtāḥ
translation: Those who without entertaining any blessings propitiate the Lord, do not wish the attainment of even Mokṣa, are declared as clever in accomplishing their objects.

devanagari: आराध्यात्मप्रदं देवं स्वात्मानं जगदीश्वरम् । को वृणीत गुणस्पर्शं बुधः स्यान्नरकेऽपि यत् ॥ ७५ ॥
english_devnagari: ārādhyātmapradaṃ devaṃ svātmānaṃ jagadīśvaramko vṛṇīta guṇasparśaṃ budhaḥ syānnarake'pi yat
translation: After propitiating the Ruler of the World who is one’s own Soul and is a bestower of his own Self, what wise man would seek contact with guṇas (worldly objects)? For (the products) the guṇas are available even in hell.

devanagari: तदिदं मम दौर्जन्यं बालिशस्य महीयसि । क्षन्तुमर्हसि मातस्त्वं दिष्ट्या गर्भो मृतोत्थितः ॥ ७६ ॥
english_devnagari: tadidaṃ mama daurjanyaṃ bāliśasya mahīyasikṣantumarhasi mātastvaṃ diṣṭyā garbho mṛtotthitaḥ
translation: Therefore, Oh Mother, it behoves you to forgive this act of wickedness on my part which I, as a fool, committed to the worshipful ones. Fortunately the foetus has become alive again (after death).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इन्द्र स्तयाभ्यनुज्ञातः शुद्धभावेन तुष्टया । मरुद्भिः सह तां नत्वा जगाम त्रिदिवं प्रभुः ॥ ७७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaindra stayābhyanujñātaḥ śuddhabhāvena tuṣṭayāmarudbhiḥ saha tāṃ natvā jagāma tridivaṃ prabhuḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Indra, the god of heaven, was permitted (to go) by her who was sincerely pleased with him. He, along with Maruts, bowed to her and went to the heaven.

devanagari: एवं ते सर्वमाख्यातं यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि । मङ्गलं मरुतां जन्म किं भूयः कथयामि ते ॥ ७८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ te sarvamākhyātaṃ yanmāṃ tvaṃ paripṛcchasimaṅgalaṃ marutāṃ janma kiṃ bhūyaḥ kathayāmi te
translation:
In this way, I have narrated to you in details what you had asked me, namely, the auspicious birth of the Maruts. Now tell me what else I should relate to you.
[back to top]
